meta.description: Browser-use plugin E2E test cases. Tests verify Claude can accomplish browser automation tasks using the browser-use MCP server tools (navigate, screenshot, click, type, etc.). Requires the browser-use plugin to be loaded and a reachable Chrome/Chromium install.
meta.notes: v1.0: Initial suite. Covers session lifecycle (create, navigate, screenshot, close). REGRESSION coverage: SingletonLock contention bug tracked in dev-fix-20260325-143046-4ff02ed6.

test_cases.description: Navigate to a URL and verify session_id is returned — basic session lifecycle smoke test
test_cases.prompt:
Use the browser-use MCP tools to do the following:
1. Call the navigate tool with url="https://example.com" (do not pass a session_id — let the server create a new one).
2. Report the session_id returned by the navigate call.
3. Take a screenshot using the screenshot tool, passing the session_id from step 2.
4. Call the close_session tool with the session_id from step 2.
5. Report whether the page title visible in the screenshot contains 'Example'.

IMPORTANT: Only use mcp__browser-use__* tools. Do NOT use any other browser automation tools.

test_cases.description: Regression: two sequential navigate calls must not time out due to SingletonLock contention — each call should succeed within the timeout window
test_cases.prompt:
Use the browser-use MCP tools to run two sequential navigation tasks that share no session, verifying that a second Chrome session can start even when one is already running:
1. Call navigate with url="https://example.com" and no session_id. Record session_id_A.
2. Call navigate with url="https://example.org" and no session_id. Record session_id_B.
3. Take a screenshot with session_id_A.
4. Take a screenshot with session_id_B.
5. Close session_id_A with close_session.
6. Close session_id_B with close_session.
7. Report both session IDs and confirm both screenshots succeeded.

IMPORTANT: Only use mcp__browser-use__* tools. Both navigate calls MUST succeed — if either times out or returns an error this test fails.
